what_it_does: A JSON batch manifest that enumerates six source files from the claude-ds project to be processed together in the wave-002 interpret/alloc pipeline.
observations: This file is a JSON array of six entries.
Each entry records a project name, a relative file path, and an absolute root directory.
All entries share the same project 'claude-ds' and root '/home/tim/company/design/claude-ds'.
The listed paths span analysis/ and app/ai/ directories.
No code symbols are present; it is purely a file inventory.
standouts: All six entries point to the same project root, making this a homogeneous batch.
It mixes analysis markdown files with app/ai JavaScript modules.
The filename 'batch-002.json' suggests it is one of a sequence of allocation batches.
conventions: Uses a consistent three-field schema: project, path, root.
concerns: No version, timestamp, or batch identifier inside the payload itself.
No indication of why these specific files were grouped together.
Absolute root path is hard-coded to a local filesystem location.
notes: A homogeneous batch inventory of six claude-ds files, mostly AI-related app modules plus two analysis documents, intended for grouped interpretation.
questions: What selection criteria grouped these particular files into batch-002?
Is the absolute root path portable across environments?
Should the batch include metadata such as priority, owner, or processing stage?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears to serve as an allocation batch manifest and does so; stated purpose is not explicitly declared, but the content matches the path naming.
intention_signals: It seems intended to feed a fixed set of claude-ds files into an interpretive analysis or indexing step as a controlled batch.
contribution: Provides a simple, reusable batch-inventory schema that a fused system could keep for grouping files by project and root during pipeline allocation.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON allocation batch listing six project-relative files from the claude-ds repository for wave-002 interpret processing.
intention_for_embedding: To define a homogeneous batch of source files to be interpreted or indexed together in the build-prep pipeline.
